---
title: Get started
---

This page installs `heormodel` and runs a first analysis on an existing PSA table. It assumes Python 3.11 or newer. For a narrated analysis, continue to the [bring your own outputs tutorial](tutorials/byo-outputs.qmd).

## Install

With [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/):

```bash
uv add heormodel
```

Or with pip:

```bash
pip install heormodel
```

The `calibration` extra adds ABC-SMC calibration via `pyabc`: `uv add "heormodel[calibration]"`.

## Bring your own outputs

You do not need a model engine to use `heormodel`. Any tidy costs/effects table with `strategy`, `iteration`, `cost`, and `qaly` columns (a spreadsheet export, a legacy simulator's output) enters the pipeline through `as_outcomes`:

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from heormodel.run import as_outcomes
from heormodel.cea import ceac, icer_table
from heormodel.voi import evpi

rng = np.random.default_rng(20260704)
n = 2_000
df = pd.concat(
    pd.DataFrame({
        "strategy": name, "iteration": range(n),
        "cost": rng.normal(cost, 1_500, n), "qaly": rng.normal(q, 0.35, n),
    })
    for name, cost, q in [("A", 30_000, 7.0), ("B", 38_000, 7.4), ("C", 55_000, 7.5)]
)

outcomes = as_outcomes(df)          # accepts a DataFrame or a CSV path
icer_table(outcomes).round(1)
```

The incremental analysis reports ICERs along the efficiency frontier; the `status` column marks dominated (`D`) and extended-dominated (`ED`) strategies, and here all three are non-dominated (`ND`). From the same object, acceptability curves and value of information:

```{python}
curves = ceac(outcomes, np.linspace(0, 100_000, 41))
curves.iloc[::10].round(3)
```

```{python}
print(f"EVPI at WTP 50,000: {evpi(outcomes, wtp=50_000):,.1f}")
```

`as_outcomes` also reads a CSV path directly, and column names are remappable via keyword arguments; see [`as_outcomes`](reference/as_outcomes.qmd).

## Next steps

- [Bring your own outputs](tutorials/byo-outputs.qmd): the same wedge, expanded to plots, EVPPI ranking, and a run report.
- [Markov cohort model](tutorials/mdm-cohort.qmd): the standard state-transition model, run through the same pipeline. The [microsimulation engine](tutorials/microsim.qmd) is the step up in detail after it.
- [Architecture](concepts/architecture.qmd): the `Outcomes` schema and the shared iteration index.
